title: App Detection Failure During ESP (0x87D1041C)
description: Detects when the Enrollment Status Page failed because Intune could not verify an app was installed (HRESULT 0x87D1041C). The installer ran but the detection rule did not match afterwards.
explanation:
App **{{appName}}** failed during the Enrollment Status Page with HRESULT **{{errorCode}}** — *Application not detected after installation completed successfully*. The installer reported success but the Intune detection rule (file/registry/script) did not find the app afterwards, so the ESP Apps subcategory failed and the device's enrollment terminated.

This is a confirmed detection-rule mismatch, NOT an install timeout — the install itself most likely completed (or the installer exited 0). Common root causes:

- The detection rule looks for a registry/file path that the installer doesn't create (wrong path, wrong bitness — e.g. checking HKLM\SOFTWARE\... on a 32-bit installer that lands under WOW6432Node).
- The installer writes the detection artefact AFTER the ESP's detection probe runs (e.g. async post-install task, requires reboot).
- The detection script returns 0 with no stdout (Intune treats empty stdout as "not detected").
- The installer wrote files to a user profile but the rule looks in System scope (or vice-versa).

remediation.title: Validate the detection rule against an installed device
steps: Open Intune portal → Apps → {{appName}} → Properties → Detection rules
Note the exact path / registry key / script the rule checks
Install the app manually on a reference device (outside Autopilot) and verify the detection artefact actually exists at that path AFTER the installer finishes
For registry rules: check both HKLM\SOFTWARE\... and HKLM\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\... depending on the installer's bitness
For file rules: ensure the path is what the installer actually writes (case, trailing version folder, ProgramData vs Program Files)
For script detection: test the script in PowerShell as SYSTEM (psexec -s) — it must write at least one line to stdout AND exit 0

remediation.title: Investigate post-install delay
steps: Check whether the installer schedules an async task (Windows Installer scheduled reboot, service start) that creates the detection artefact only after ESP completes
If yes: either move the detection artefact creation into the synchronous installer path, or add a wait step in the installer wrapper script before exit
As a workaround, change the detection rule to something the synchronous installer writes immediately (typically the MSI ProductCode under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Uninstall\)

remediation.title: Cross-check the agent's per-app event
steps: Look at the app_install_failed event in this session for DataJson.detectionResult — NotDetected confirms Intune's detection probe ran and returned negative
Look at app_install_failed.bytesDownloaded vs bytesTotal — if they match, the package downloaded fully (install error vs. download error)
Cross-reference DataJson.exitCode and hresultFromWin32 — these come from the installer itself and may explain why the detection artefact was not written
